campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and recruitment efforts for city positions. The council also addressed public input on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on council reports
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to use for the next fiscal year starting in 2026. The council needed to pick from different service level proposals and then provide direction on the city's approach to sheriff services going forward.
keyTopics: Selecting sheriff service level options for FY 2026
City council decision on near-term sheriff services approach
Evaluating different proposed service options from the Sheriff

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve financial reports, authorize the town manager to sign several multi-year service agreements and construction contracts, and apply for state grant funding. The council also adopted a list of projects for the next fiscal year that will be funded by state grants.
keyTopics: March 2026 financial and investment report
Five-year service agreement with HouseKeys
Construction contract with O'Grady Paving
State grant applications for unspecified statewide program
Fiscal Year 2026-27 project list and state funding

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 16, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve routine administrative items including committee minutes and board appointments, settle a legal claim involving Gordon Biersch Brewing Company, and make decisions about a downtown hotel assessment district and a property acquisition on South First Street.
keyTopics: Settlement of a government claim with Gordon Biersch Brewing Company
Annual report and assessment levy for the Hotel Business Improvement District for 2026-2027
Real property acquisition at 26-36 South First Street
Appointments to city boards and commissions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a closed session on June 23, 2026 to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and to meet with legal counsel about an ongoing lawsuit involving water quality issues and a separate legal matter with potential significant financial risk.
keyTopics: City Attorney recruitment and appointment process
San Francisco Baykeeper lawsuit against Mountain View and Sunnyvale
Anticipated litigation with significant financial exposure

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The closed session typically addresses confidential personnel, legal, or financial matters that cannot be discussed publicly.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting
Regular council business and public hearings
Standard council meeting procedures

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 15, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting with legal counsel to discuss an anticipated lawsuit that the city may initiate. The council also reviewed what was discussed in that private session during an open meeting portion.
keyTopics: One pending litigation case being considered for filing by the city
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 23, 2026, using both in-person and remote participation options. Residents could attend in person or join via Zoom, and had the ability to submit written public comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission through eComments system
Remote participation access via Zoom or phone

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve updates to the city's federal funding plans for 2025-2026, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and handle routine meeting administration. Council members also submitted requests for new items to be added to future workplans.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 workplan (federal funding priorities)
Proposed changes to the Council Vacancy Ordinance
New workplan item requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and calendar of upcoming meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing, and the developer is asking for special allowances under state law to make the project work on the small parcel.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on 0.43-acre lot at 450 Lytton Avenue
Six-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers under state law
Streamlined review process for housing development
